nodes.label: AI as qualitative data gatherer for credit write-ups
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or insufficient for qualitative analysis
nodes.grounding_span: I also use LLMs to help me gather qualitative information to complete large credit write ups

nodes.label: AI effectiveness dependent on industry-specific customization
nodes.label_negative: Generic AI models misaligned with business needs
nodes.grounding_span: for an Ai model to be truly effective in the financial sector, it would be best closely developed by the company that is going to use it

nodes.label: Human instinct and qualitative judgment in risk analysis
nodes.grounding_span: human instinct or qualitative factors that the models are not great at accounting for yet

nodes.label: AI handling of routine customer tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I wish I could get AI to handle that would free up my time to do other things- like dealing with customers who need small tasks

nodes.label: Privacy and regulatory compliance in AI use
nodes.label_negative: AI use compromising privacy or regulations
nodes.grounding_span: consumer privacy and having to make sure they follow government regulations surrounding privacy

nodes.label: Workflow optimization and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: it would optimize our workflow and maybe even mitigate some loss

nodes.label: Professional competence and risk mitigation
nodes.grounding_span: I have to run risk rating models that depend on my human inputs and calculations

nodes.label: Client privacy and trust
nodes.grounding_span: consumer privacy and having to make sure they follow government regulations

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for qualitative research
nodes.grounding_span: I asked about Hampstead's population growth... and even what occupancy rates looked like

nodes.label: Frustration with generic risk rating model
nodes.grounding_span: The risk rating model that I was referring to can be really frustrating

nodes.label: Optimism about internally developed AI
nodes.grounding_span: I would love to find out that my company has invested heavily into developing a model

nodes.label: Confidence in job security due to human judgment
nodes.grounding_span: I think that because of the human instinct factor of risk analysis we discussed, my job is safe

nodes.label: Segmented iterative questioning — breaks research into sequential queries
nodes.grounding_span: I ask the questions in segments, because I am structuring my write-ups

nodes.label: Privacy-conscious compartmentalization — sanitizes queries to avoid client identification
nodes.grounding_span: I have to be very careful only to include information that cannot be tied back to our client in my queries
